results.query: I just finished a chunk of work on the billing module — can you acceptance-test my changes before I push?
results.note: Core: change-driven E2E on recent work, before push gate.

results.query: Did my latest commits break any user flows?
results.note: Core question-form: diff-driven regression check across flows.

results.query: Run E2E on whatever I changed in this branch and tell me if anything regressed.
results.note: Core: detect diff -> map to flows -> run; explicit regression intent.

results.query: Before I open the PR, validate my changes end to end so I know I didn't break checkout or login.
results.note: Core: pre-PR acceptance gate; change-driven over the diff.

results.query: Make sure I didn't break anything with the work I did today.
results.note: Core: 'didn't break anything' on 'my work' -> change-driven acceptance.

results.query: Test my changes against the staging URL https://staging.acme.app before we merge.
results.note: Core: change-driven run on staging/deployed URL, pre-merge.

results.query: Regression test my work on the preview deploy at https://pr-482.preview.vercel.app
results.note: Core: run on preview URL, change-driven regression of own work.

results.query: I refactored the auth context and touched a bunch of components — can you figure out what flows that affects and run acceptance tests?
results.note: Core: diff -> affected flows mapping is explicit; whole-change validation.

results.query: Can you check that my recent changes didn't regress the signup and onboarding flows before I push this branch?
results.note: Core: multiple flows but anchored on 'recent changes' + before push -> muggle-test, not feature-local.

results.query: About to raise a PR for the cart redesign work. Run the acceptance suite on my diff first.
results.note: Core: pre-PR gate, 'my diff', acceptance suite run.

results.query: I rebased and squashed a few commits — re-run the E2E acceptance tests on what I changed and post the results to the PR.
results.note: Core: change-driven run with PR result publishing as part of the run.

results.query: Deploy preview is live for my branch. Test the changes I made on that preview URL and let me know if any flow is broken.
results.note: Core: test-on-preview, change-driven, broken-flow detection.

results.query: I touched the API client and a couple of pages this afternoon. Did I break the user-facing flows?
results.note: Core question-form: 'did I break' tied to today's changes across flows.

results.query: Validate everything I've been working on this sprint before the merge to main.
results.note: Core: pre-merge acceptance over accumulated changes / work.

results.query: Run change-driven E2E on my branch versus main and flag any broken acceptance flows.
results.note: Core: explicit diff branch-vs-main, change-driven E2E framing.

results.query: Acceptance-test my work on staging before I cut the release.
results.note: Core: own work + staging URL + pre-release gate.

results.query: Quick sanity acceptance run on my changes before I push — focus on whatever my edits could have affected.
results.note: Core: before push, scoped to the diff's blast radius.

results.query: I think my last few commits might have broken something in the dashboard. Can you run the E2E acceptance tests to confirm?
results.note: Core: suspected regression from recent commits -> run acceptance to confirm.

results.query: validate my changes before I open the PR
results.note: HARD (vs verification-before-completion): 'validate my changes before the PR' means run the acceptance E2E suite, not a completion checklist.

results.query: Before I mark this done and open the PR, can you verify my changes actually work end to end in the browser?
results.note: HARD (vs verification-before-completion): 'verify before done/PR' but explicitly browser E2E -> acceptance run, not the completion-claim checklist.

results.query: Check my changes before the PR goes up — run the real acceptance flows, don't just eyeball it.
results.note: HARD (vs verification-before-completion): 'check my changes before PR' disambiguated to actually running acceptance flows.

results.query: I'm about to claim this is fixed and push it. Run the acceptance suite on my changes so I have evidence the flows still work.
results.note: HARD (vs verification-before-completion): completion-claim vocabulary but the ask is to execute the acceptance suite over the diff.

results.query: I changed how the login form submits — but treat this as 'test my changes', whatever else my diff touched should get tested too.
results.note: HARD (vs muggle-test-feature-local): mentions one feature yet anchors on 'test my changes / whole diff' -> stays muggle-test.

results.query: I edited the checkout component plus some shared utils. Don't just test checkout — test everything my changes could have broken before I push.
results.note: HARD (vs muggle-test-feature-local): named feature present but intent is whole-diff/before-push -> muggle-test.

results.query: Run acceptance tests on the work I did before pushing — I poked at the profile page but also moved some routing logic around.
results.note: HARD (vs muggle-test-feature-local): named page mentioned but anchored on 'my work / before pushing' across the change set.

results.query: Here's my branch with the password-reset fix and a few other edits. Before I push, run E2E on my changes and tell me if I regressed any flow.
results.note: HARD (vs muggle-test-feature-local): single named fix plus 'other edits', anchored on 'my changes / before push / any flow' -> change-driven muggle-test.

results.query: Test the signup flow on localhost:3000 and make sure a brand-new user can register all the way through.
results.note: Core entrance: named flow (signup) + localhost port, verify it works.

results.query: Verify the new checkout works in a real browser — add an item, enter card details, and confirm the order goes through on http://localhost:5173.
results.note: Core: named flow (checkout) on dev URL, real-browser verification.

results.query: Click through the password reset flow on my dev server and confirm a user can actually get back into their account.
results.note: Core: named flow (password reset), 'click through ... confirm it works'.

results.query: Can you run a real-browser test of the onboarding wizard on localhost:4200? I want to know the multi-step setup completes cleanly.
results.note: Core: named flow (onboarding wizard) + localhost port, real browser.

results.query: Test the cart quantity controls on localhost:3000 — bump the qty up and down and verify the line total and subtotal update correctly.
results.note: Core: named feature (cart qty), localhost, verify behavior.

results.query: I just wired up search. Verify the search feature works on http://localhost:8080 — type a query and confirm relevant results show up.
results.note: Core: named feature (search) on dev URL, verify it works (a test, not change-driven diff).

results.query: Spin up a browser against localhost:3001 and confirm the file upload feature works end to end — pick a file, upload, and check it appears in the list.
results.note: Core: named feature (file upload), localhost, real browser E2E.

results.query: Walk through the login flow on the dev server and make sure valid credentials get me to the dashboard.
results.note: Core: named flow (login), dev server, verify lands on dashboard.

results.query: Verify the add-to-cart button works on localhost:5000 and that the cart badge count increments each time.
results.note: Core: named feature (add to cart), localhost, verify increment.

results.query: Test the multi-step booking flow on localhost:3000 — pick a date, choose a slot, and confirm the reservation lands on the confirmation screen.
results.note: Core: named multi-step flow (booking), localhost, verify confirmation.

results.query: Can you test the contact form on http://localhost:8000 in a real browser — fill it out, submit, and verify the success message appears?
results.note: Core: named feature (contact form), dev URL, verify success state (validation/UI behavior).

results.query: Run an end-to-end browser check of the subscription upgrade flow on localhost:3000 and confirm a free user can move to the Pro plan.
results.note: Core: named flow (subscription upgrade), localhost, E2E verify.

results.query: Verify the dark mode toggle works on my dev server — flip it and confirm the whole UI switches themes and the setting sticks on reload.
results.note: Core: named UI feature (dark mode toggle), dev server, verify behavior persists.

results.query: Test the new comment thread feature on localhost:4000 — post a comment, reply to it, and make sure both render in order.
results.note: Core: named feature (comment thread), localhost, verify rendering.

results.query: Click through the guest checkout flow on http://localhost:5173 and confirm someone can buy without creating an account.
results.note: Core: named flow (guest checkout), dev URL, 'click through ... confirm'.

results.query: Verify the two-factor login works in a real browser on localhost:3000 — enter the code prompt and confirm it lets me into the app.
results.note: Core: named flow (2FA login), localhost, real-browser verify.

results.query: Test the profile edit feature on the dev server — change the display name and avatar, save, and confirm the changes persist.
results.note: Core: named feature (profile edit), dev server, verify persistence.

results.query: Run a browser test of the coupon code feature at localhost:5000 — apply a valid code at checkout and confirm the discount is reflected in the total.
results.note: Core: named feature (coupon code), localhost, verify discount applied.

results.query: Verify the new signup form's validation works on localhost:3000 — try a weak password and a bad email and confirm the inline errors show.
results.note: HARD vs muggle-test: anchored on a SPECIFIC named feature (signup validation) on localhost, not 'my changes/diff'.

results.query: I want to confirm the export-to-CSV button on the reports page actually works on http://localhost:8080 — click it and verify a file downloads with the right rows.
results.note: HARD vs muggle-do-task: intent is to VERIFY the export works (a test), not merely perform the export.

results.query: Test the 'forgot username' lookup flow on localhost:3000 and make sure entering my email surfaces the right account hint.
results.note: HARD vs muggle-test: a specific named flow (forgot username), local; not change/diff-driven.

results.query: Log into the demo account on the dev server and verify the whole posting flow works — compose, attach an image, publish, and confirm the post appears in the feed.
results.note: HARD vs muggle-do-task: superficially 'log in and post', but the stated goal is to VERIFY the posting flow works end to end.

results.query: Verify the infinite scroll on the product listing page works on localhost:5173 — scroll down and confirm more items load without duplicates.
results.note: HARD vs muggle-test: specific named UI behavior (infinite scroll) on localhost, feature-anchored not change-anchored.

results.query: Can you confirm the address autocomplete in the checkout form works on localhost:3000 — start typing a street and verify suggestions appear and one can be selected?
results.note: HARD vs muggle-do-task: filling the form is the vehicle, but the intent is to VERIFY autocomplete works.

results.query: Test the role-based access on the admin settings page on http://localhost:4000 — log in as a regular user and confirm the admin tab is hidden.
results.note: HARD vs muggle-test: a specific named feature (RBAC on admin page) on localhost; verification of behavior, not a diff test.

results.query: Verify the new drag-and-drop reorder on the dashboard works on the dev server — drag a card to a new position and confirm the order saves after refresh.
results.note: HARD vs muggle-do-task: dragging is the action, but intent is to VERIFY the reorder feature works and persists.

results.query: muggle upgrade
results.note: Explicit canonical trigger — bare upgrade command.

results.query: Can you update muggle to the latest version for me?
results.note: Direct update-to-latest intent.

results.query: Is there a newer version of muggle available? If so, grab it.
results.note: "Is there a newer muggle" + pull it down.

results.query: Pull down the latest muggle release please.
results.note: Get the newer version of Muggle itself.

results.query: Am I on the latest muggle? If not, bump me up.
results.note: "Am I on the latest muggle" + upgrade if behind.

results.query: Upgrade the muggle tooling to whatever the current version is.
results.note: "Upgrade the muggle tooling" explicit.

results.query: I think my muggle is out of date — update it.
results.note: Out-of-date Muggle, update intent.

results.query: Update the muggle CLI to the newest build.
results.note: "Upgrade the muggle CLI" phrasing.

results.query: Get me the latest muggle, mine feels old.
results.note: Latest muggle, casual register.

results.query: Run an upgrade on muggle so I'm current.
results.note: Upgrade muggle to be current.

results.query: There's a new muggle version out, right? Install it.
results.note: New version exists, install the newer Muggle.

results.query: Bump muggle to the latest and greatest.
results.note: Bump Muggle itself to latest.

results.note: Update muggle, do-it intent.

results.note: Upgrade the muggle plugin to latest.

results.note: Refresh/update Muggle to current release.

results.query: Check for muggle updates and apply them.

results.note: HARD: dep-file vocabulary but unambiguously upgrading Muggle itself, not generic deps.

results.query: Everything in my project is on the latest except muggle — bring muggle up to date too.
results.note: HARD: tempting general-update framing, but the target is explicitly muggle.

results.query: I just ran npm update on my app deps; separately, can you also upgrade the muggle tooling to the latest version?
results.note: HARD: mentions npm update (negative-class lure) but the actual ask is to upgrade Muggle specifically.

results.query: When I bump my other dev dependencies I always forget muggle — go ahead and update muggle to the current version.
results.note: HARD: 'dev dependencies' near-boundary phrasing, but the request is squarely upgrade Muggle.

results.query: Don't touch my project's packages, just upgrade muggle itself to the latest.
results.note: HARD: explicitly excludes generic dep upgrades, isolates Muggle upgrade.

results.query: Is the muggle CLI I'm running outdated compared to the published release? Update it if so.
results.note: HARD: 'published release' could read like a works release, but it's about whether the user's Muggle CLI is behind and upgrading it.

results.query: muggle status
results.note: Explicit canonical trigger — bare status command.

results.query: Can you run muggle status for me real quick?
results.note: Explicit status request, casual phrasing.

results.query: Is my Muggle install healthy right now?
results.note: Direct health-check intent.

results.query: Check whether the Muggle MCP is connected properly.
results.note: MCP connection health check.

results.query: Is my muggle auth still valid or did my login expire?
results.note: Auth/login validity check.

results.query: I want to make sure muggle is set up correctly before I start testing.
results.note: "Is it set up right" verification intent.

results.query: Give me a full health report on my Muggle Test setup.
results.note: Health report = diagnose/report, status.

results.query: Are the muggle MCP tools actually loading? Want to confirm they're live.
results.note: MCP health, confirm not fix.

results.query: Did my Muggle session token expire? Check my auth please.
results.note: Auth validity diagnose.

results.query: Verify that everything in my muggle environment is wired up right.
results.note: Setup correctness check.

results.query: Can you confirm Muggle is talking to the backend okay?
results.note: Connectivity/health confirmation.

results.query: What's the current state of my muggle installation — all green?
results.note: Status report phrasing.

results.query: I just installed muggle. Can you check it's configured properly?
results.note: Post-install setup verification.

results.query: Run a quick diagnostic on muggle and tell me if anything looks off.
results.note: Diagnose-only intent, report findings.

results.query: Is the muggle MCP server reachable from here? Just checking.
results.note: MCP reachability check, diagnose.

results.query: muggle's been acting up today — can you take a look and tell me what's wrong?
results.note: HARD near-boundary: ambiguous "acting up" + diagnose intent, leans status (diagnose before fix).

results.query: Muggle isn't responding when I try to run tests. Is something broken with it?
results.note: HARD near-boundary: "is something broken?" is diagnose, not "fix it" → status.

results.query: The MCP keeps timing out — can you figure out if it's a muggle health issue?
results.note: HARD near-boundary: investigate cause vs repair; intent is diagnose → status.

results.query: I think my muggle login might be busted. Can you check before I do anything?
results.note: HARD near-boundary: suspects breakage but asks to CHECK first → status.

results.query: Muggle commands are failing silently. Is it me or is the install unhealthy?
results.note: HARD near-boundary: "is the install unhealthy?" diagnose framing → status.

results.query: Something feels off with muggle — diagnose it for me, don't fix anything yet.
results.note: HARD near-boundary: explicit diagnose-only, defer fixing → status.

results.query: Before I report a bug, can you tell me if my muggle auth and MCP are healthy?
results.note: Health check of auth + MCP, report intent.

results.query: Why does muggle keep saying it can't connect? Check its status for me.
results.note: Connectivity symptom + explicit status check.

results.query: Quick sanity check — is muggle good to go or is there a problem with the setup?
results.note: Sanity/health check, diagnose whether setup is okay.

results.query: run npm test and tell me which jest specs are failing in src/utils/date.test.ts

results.query: this vitest test is flaky on CI — sometimes passes, sometimes times out. can you figure out why?

results.query: muggle repair
results.note: Canonical explicit invocation of the repair skill.

results.query: Run muggle repair, my setup is hosed.
results.note: Explicit 'muggle repair' plus broken-setup framing.

results.query: My muggle install is completely busted, can you fix it?
results.note: Plain 'busted, fix it' broken-install repair intent.

results.query: The muggle MCP server won't load in Claude — please repair the install.
results.note: MCP won't load -> explicit repair request.

results.query: Reinstall the muggle tooling for me, it's broken.
results.note: Reinstall/repair tooling, broken install.

results.query: Something corrupted my muggle setup after the last update. Fix it.
results.note: Corrupted setup, direct fix command.

results.query: Claude says the muggle MCP failed to connect. Can you get it working again?
results.note: MCP failed to connect -> restore/fix intent.

results.query: Please repair my broken Muggle Test installation.
results.note: Explicit 'repair' + 'broken installation'.

results.query: muggle keeps erroring on startup — reinstall and repair the whole thing.
results.note: Startup errors, reinstall + repair tooling.

results.query: The muggle CLI is throwing 'command not found' even though I installed it. Fix the install.
results.note: Broken binary/PATH, explicit fix the install.

results.query: My muggle MCP shows up red/disconnected in Claude — go ahead and fix it.
results.note: MCP disconnected -> explicit go-ahead to fix (not just report).

results.query: I think my muggle config got wiped. Rebuild the install so it works again.
results.note: Wiped config -> rebuild/repair the install.

results.query: Repair muggle — the electron app and MCP are both refusing to start.
results.note: Explicit repair, multiple components failing.

results.query: Nuke and reinstall muggle, the current install is beyond fixing.
results.note: Reinstall tooling from scratch, repair-class action.

results.query: After upgrading my OS the muggle MCP stopped loading. Get it loading again, please.
results.note: MCP won't load post-OS-change -> restore/repair.

results.query: muggle's broken and I have no idea why — just repair it.
results.note: Vague brokenness BUT explicit 'just repair it' so it doesn't collapse to status.

results.query: The muggle MCP won't connect — diagnose the cause and then fix it for me.
results.note: HARD vs status: includes diagnose, but the operative ask is the fix; repair owns diagnose-then-fix.

results.query: muggle status said the MCP is unhealthy. Now actually repair it.
results.note: HARD vs status: status already ran/reported; user now wants the fix action -> repair.

results.query: Don't just tell me what's wrong with muggle — go fix whatever's broken.
results.note: HARD vs status: explicitly rejects report-only, demands the fix -> repair.

results.query: muggle's acting up and the MCP keeps dropping. Find the problem and repair the install.
results.note: HARD vs status: 'acting up' would lean status, but 'repair the install' pins it to repair.

results.query: Check why the muggle MCP isn't loading and then reinstall whatever's broken to fix it.
results.note: HARD vs status: check is subordinate to reinstall/fix the broken piece -> repair.

results.query: Is muggle broken? If so, fix it right now — don't make me run another command.
results.note: HARD vs status: starts as a check but the action intent is fix-now -> repair.

results.query: My muggle auth and MCP both broke at once — repair the whole installation.
results.note: Multiple subsystems broken, explicit repair the installation.

results.query: Whatever broke in my muggle install during the npm update, undo it and get muggle working again.
results.note: Broken-by-update install, repair/restore to working state.
